matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Exodus 5:20:1
matches.x: AND THEY MET. The words “who were” are missing.129Our verse literally reads: And they met Moses and Aaron standing. J.P.S. renders: And they met Moses and Aaron, who stood in the way. Our text should be read as if written: who were standing.130Our verse should be read: And they met Moses and Aaron who were standing. And Pharaoh dreams (Gen. 41:1)131Translated according to I.E. is similar. Its…

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Exodus 5:21:2
matches.x: [YE HAVE MADE OUR SAVOUR TO BE ABHORRED IN THE EYES OF PHARAOH.] Note that the five senses are perceived in one place located above the forehead.134Cf. I.E.’s comments on Gen. 42:1 and the notes thereto. Hence one sense is substituted for another sense,135Scripture should have read: Ye have caused our savor to be abhorred in the nose of Pharaoh, for it is the nose that detects smell. However,…

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Exodus 5:21:2
matches.x: [YE HAVE MADE OUR SAVOUR TO BE ABHORRED IN THE EYES OF PHARAOH.] Note that the five senses are perceived in one place located above the forehead.134Cf. Hence one sense is substituted for another sense,135Scripture should have read: Ye have caused our savor to be abhorred in the nose of Pharaoh, for it is the nose that detects smell. However,…
This thing [plan] was good in the eyes of Pharaoh, and in the eyes of all his servants.

The news was heard in Pharaoh’s house that Yoseif’s brothers had come. This was good [news] in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants.
